free_text: The 48Ti(a,n)51Cr and 48Ti(a,p)51V cross sections
5U Pelletron accelerator
Two 48Ti targets of different thicknesses were prepared
by evaporation from an intimate mixture of a powdered
deducing agent and TiO2 enriched in 48Ti, onto 0.13 mm
gold backings. The reducing agent used in the
preparation of the thicker target was graphite and the
48Ti enrichment 99.65%, while the other the reducing
agent was tantalum and the 48Ti enrichment 99.81%.
Target thicknesses were: (4.1+-0.2)x10**18 and
(7.3+-0.4)x10**17 48Ti atoms/cm2, respectively.
Data were received from the author: S.G.Tims
and from table 2.
SD
Two Ge(Li) detectors, which had active volumes
125 cm3 and 65 cm3, were placed in the +-90 degr.
directions
The 48Ti(a,n) yield was
measured by observation of the 27.7d 51Cr activity by
means of the 320 keV 1->0 gamma-ray in 51V from
C.Zhou, etc. Nuclear Data Sheets, v.48, p.111, 1986
Error in the cross section scale, the main
contributors bing target thickness (5%), beam current
integration (5%), gamma-ray efficiency (6%) and the
51Cr decay branching ratio (2%).
Statistical error
Thicker of two targets was used
A surface-barrier detector in the 125 degr.
direction.
Error in the cross section scale, the main
contributors bing target thickness (5%), beam current
integration (5%), detector solid angle (4%) and the
angular distributions correction (2%).
Statistical error
Thinner of two targets was used
